description: Curated AI coding agent safety rules covering git, rm, find, xargs, and shell wrapper dangerous patterns

rules.reason: git checkout -- discards uncommitted changes permanently. Use git stash first.
git checkout --force discards uncommitted changes. Use git stash first.
git checkout <ref> -- <path> overwrites working tree with ref version.
git switch --discard-changes discards uncommitted changes. Use git stash first.
git switch --force discards uncommitted changes. Use git stash first.
git restore discards uncommitted changes. Use git stash first, or use --staged to only unstage.
git restore --worktree explicitly discards working tree changes. Use git stash first.
git reset --hard destroys all uncommitted changes permanently. Use git stash first.
git reset --merge can lose uncommitted changes. Use git stash first.
git clean -f removes untracked files permanently. Use git clean -n to preview first.
git push --force destroys remote history. Use --force-with-lease for safer force push.
git branch -D force-deletes without merge check. Use -d for safe delete.
git stash drop permanently deletes stashed changes. Consider git stash list first.
git stash clear deletes ALL stashed changes permanently.
git worktree remove --force can delete uncommitted changes. Remove --force flag.
rm -rf targeting root directory is extremely dangerous and always blocked.
rm -rf targeting home directory is extremely dangerous and always blocked.
rm -rf targeting home directory is extremely dangerous and always blocked.
rm -rf targeting cwd itself. Use explicit paths within the current directory.
rm -rf outside cwd is blocked. Use explicit paths or delete manually.
rm -rf in temporary directory is safe (tmp files are meant to be deleted).
rm -rf in temporary directory is safe.
find -delete removes files without confirmation. Use find -print first to verify targets.
find -exec rm can execute arbitrary deletion on many files simultaneously.
find -exec sh allows arbitrary command execution across multiple files.
xargs rm -rf can delete unpredictable targets based on stdin content.
xargs sh -c allows arbitrary command execution with unpredicted arguments.
bash -c wrapping dangerous command bypasses simple pattern matching.
sh -c wrapping dangerous command bypasses simple pattern matching.
env wrapper bypasses command denylist by prefixing a dangerous command.
sudo + destructive operation combination is extremely dangerous.
sudo + git destructive operation may affect system files.
watch repeating destructive command amplifies damage.
ionice wrapper may hide I/O activity of destructive commands.
setsid wrapping destructive command detaches from terminal control.
chroot escape attempt via temporary directory bind mount.
bind mount of root filesystem for container/jail escape.
